title: Viewing Parent Item of a Subitem on My Work
excerpt: In My Work, subitems show up with details of the board they’re on (Subitems of [board name]) and what group they are a part of. Depending on how you have your board set up, the group can be useless sometimes. Can we plea…

posts.author: akrukemyer (Adam Krukemyer)
posts.cookedHtml: In My Work, subitems show up with details of the board they’re on (Subitems of [board name]) and what group they are a part of. Depending on how you have your board set up, the group can be useless sometimes. Can we please see the Parent Item of the Subitem on My Work?

posts.author: elcloche (Alejandro Graffe)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is a must. I don’t know how this has so little votes.

I have an automation that creates several subitems, so there are many subitems with the same name and whenever we see those subitems in “My work” we can not diferenciate one from another.

I also tried to change the name of the subitem within the automation but there is no way to dinamicaly change the name depending on the parent.

posts.author: loisa (Loisa Matthys)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have this same issue. I’m also perplexed that more don’t have the issue. I have automations creating workflows when triggered, all named the same thing and only differentiated by their parent item. When trying to set dependencies, there is literally no way to know which subitem belongs to which parent item. Even if I expand each one, they only show the group and not the parent item. Please, please update – seems like it would be such an easy fix.

Screen Shot 2022-06-29 at 11.35.22 AM798×910 70.2 KB

posts.author: kvilika (Kudzai)
posts.cookedHtml:
I just noticed this items is duplicated here and has 13 votes. So total 20 to date:

My Work view: When listing subitems, show parent item name next to them Feature requests
Currently My Work view shows the list of tasks, where items and subitems appear as equal. The problem is, that if subitem name does not contain information from its parent item, it is hard to tell where the subitem is related. Example: Board: Videos Item: 3 ways to get started with L&D Subitem: cover image In the My Work view, the subitem is listed as “cover image” and the user needs to navigate all the way to the video board and then locate the correct parent item for the “Cover image” to …

posts.author: Davmon (David)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree with this request for the reasons stated. My work listing is of no use without links to parent items

posts.author: UMW (Gilles Cavin)
posts.cookedHtml:
There is another request opened with the same suggestion, it is a year old.
I don’t understand why it is taking so long to fix such a basic and obvious issue.

posts.author: TaylorTedesco (Taylor Tedesco)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is an issue for our team across pretty much all aspects of subitems. When we are working 60+ real estate contracts at a time and assigning tasks/subitems to people, they need to be able to see which property/parent item name the notification is coming from. It is not practical to expect someone to click into the link and open Monday just to find out, we just don’t have time for it.

Below is a test/example of what “My Week” would look like with just a fraction of the actual tasks assigned and also how it comes through on my daily highlights.

image645×750 76.7 KB

This issue persists when we want to communicate within a subitem. We have to reference the parent item name at the beginning of our message so that the notification includes it when it sends out. As we grow larger there is just no way to function with how it currently is.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is an issue for everything … it looks like paren item board and subitem board are different and not related, because the parent item name never its shown on top of the subitem name.
We need this fixed too…

posts.author: synergy (Michael Schellinger)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed, it’s cumbersome at best to navigate back to the parent item to see what, or in our case what deal, the subitem pertains to. On the mobile it is even harder…

posts.author: kamedlin (Keith Medlin)
posts.cookedHtml: There are multiple suggestions open about this very topic that all have 5+ votes. I think this should be prioritized as an item because it’s crippling the usefulness of the “My Week” summary.

posts.author: cberman (Corey)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 this is definitely needed to improve the usefulness of subitems. Right now the subitem does not get any “context” of the parent item, really the context is the whole purpose of using a subitem.

Other similar threads:
/#/t/24893

Currently My Work view shows the list of tasks, where items and subitems appear as equal. The problem is, that if subitem name does not contain information from its parent item, it is hard to tell where the subitem is related. Example: Board: Videos Item: 3 ways to get started with L&D Subitem: cover image In the My Work view, the subitem is listed as “cover image” and the user needs to navigate all the way to the video board and then locate the correct parent item for the “Cover image” to …

posts.author: TaylorTedesco (Taylor Tedesco)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates here? I don’t see anything like this on the sub-items road map and it’s crazy to me to think it’s not being worked on. What about notifications from updates with sub-items? Not receiving an item name attached to the notification makes them useless for us.

posts.author: DreydonR (Dreydon Reimer)
posts.cookedHtml: I have the same problem

posts.author: IanDirnfeld (IanDee)
posts.cookedHtml: Definitely +1 here. Any way to make this a prioroty?

posts.author: TaylorTedesco (Taylor Tedesco)
posts.cookedHtml: Doubt it. They don’t even respond about it. Seems to be it would be a pretty glaring issue but I guess not. We have to remind everyone who posts in subitems to always reference the address. It’s super annoying and inefficient.

posts.author: Michael-PT (Michael Albers)
posts.cookedHtml:
Completely agree there needs to be a better connection between parents and subs.

Additionally, it could be very useful when in a formula and especially Emails & Activities you could use something {parent.id.columnname} or {subitem.id.columnname}.

This way you could reference from parent to any subitem and from any subitem to any parent or other subitem.

posts.author: DMC (Dereine Coleman)
posts.cookedHtml: Crazy, we’re having the same issue. We are working on 20 projects with 109 subitems each, and everything looks the same and is a waste of time to click the “see all link” to determine which project needs attention. I sent an email to support but to no avail. If anyone discovers a “workaround” please share. I am considering pulling the plug on our subscription based on this alone.

posts.author: ckarpiuk (Charles Karpiuk)
posts.cookedHtml: (post deleted by author)

posts.author: ckarpiuk (Charles Karpiuk)
posts.cookedHtml: I wish this was a joke.

posts.author: Mariolsen (Mari Olsen)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is also something we’d like to see. Any update on this, Monday?

@Julietteb - who would be the best to reach out to for an answer to this?

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml: Hey! everyone! We are working on improving our new features request process but keep voting for these features and if enough people vote we can try to bring it to our product team and see what they say. Not sure on the status of this, but can try to find out

posts.author: SusannaGP1977 (Susanna G Phillips)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Julietteb +1 vote. We have over 100 projects and we would like for the subitem to show the parent name please. We need to see the parent name of the subitem when you filter by person, in My Work etc.
In My Work even if you click on the subitem it still doesn’t show you the parent name. And even if I click to take me to the main board it still doesn’t show to which parent name the subitem belongs to.

posts.author: Geratius (Geratius)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree! In our team we work on many projects and in some cases we got subelements and I see only mention to “item 1” or “item blue” and I do not know what is the parent name. I think it should be in another row or maybe in brackets but has to be mentioned in notifications for sure. Very needed upgrade!

posts.author: TaylorTedesco (Taylor Tedesco)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Juliette - any info on if this is on the roadmap? Seems like such a basic need, especially if you all are going to start targeting larger companies with likely much more data and projects to keep organized.

posts.author: JSambolin (Jennifer Sambolin)
posts.cookedHtml:
I completely agree and have voted on this same issue in many topics. But to add to the importance of the parent name in the subitem (or anything at this point to indicate which parent item the subitem correlates to!!), we have several boards that we archive the subitem tasks in order to save space once we are done with the “file”/item. Well, if you want to ever restore the subitem to see the updates etc. how do you know which one pertains to the item? I literally had to restore every single subitem I had to then get a hold of the right one. But just like MyWork, the Archive History info also doesn’t show what parent item a subitem belongs to!

image1562×767 91.3 KB

posts.author: jonofrio61 (Josee Onofrio)
posts.cookedHtml:
Would it be possible to add the Parent Record on the popup giving the details of a SubItem?

image1316×648 73.2 KB

posts.author: EmilyH (Emily Hagen)
posts.cookedHtml: When a subitem is assigned to a person, it shows up in their “My Work” view with only the subitem name. The names of subitems often don’t make sense without the parent item for context, and right now it is very tedious to have to go from My Work to the Main Board to view the parent item. These subitems should show the main item name as well in My Work.

posts.author: SMarti (Martin)
posts.cookedHtml: yes - please. Subitems are usually hard to put in context without seeing the parent. richt now if have to do a click for each item to see the parent. please show it directly in the overview

posts.author: IanDirnfeld (IanDee)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for sure. Crucial

posts.author: Regan (Regan Ludwar)
posts.cookedHtml: 100% Parent item needs to be it’s own Column please!!!

posts.author: Sonya (Sonya Senkowsky)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s also duplicated here: My Work Customization: Add or hide ANY column to view & show parent Item name for Sub items

posts.author: Sonya (Sonya Senkowsky)
posts.cookedHtml:
Just discovered a way to see “parent” items in the My Work view:
Subitems (tasks) that belong to a parent Item (“Project”) in the My Work view have a tiny subitem indicator icon next to them. Click this to see the parent items. Similarly, Parent items have a number in gray next to them; if you click this, you reveal the associated subitems.

This is FAR from intuitive and not the best solution, but it is far better than nothing!

posts.author: Sonya (Sonya Senkowsky)
posts.cookedHtml: It also looks like the pop up will summarize your columns, so we could probably create an automation that populates a column with the parent name. (Another workaround, yay.)

posts.author: InbalHorn (Inbal Horn )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi community! Thanks for all your feedback.

I am a product manager at monday.com and me and my team have been reading all your suggestions as we work on improving the “my work” feature.
I am excited to share that with the new my work improvements, you will be able see the parent item on top of each subitem at a glance. The new layout also presents the relevant board and group above the item instead of as a column, which saves a lot of the horizontal space and lets you better see the priority, date and status columns.

While this feature is in beta, we would love to get your feedback, since we know this has been one of the biggest request coming from our community and you have all given very useful feedback.
How can I access this feature? You can add this feature by going to monday labs and activating “new my work layout”.

new-layout2600×1538 339 KB

posts.author: Regan (Regan Ludwar)
posts.cookedHtml: Love it!!!

posts.author: meganmansfield (Megan Mansfield)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you so much for this! This is a game changer!!!

posts.author: AndreaMegawatt (Andrea)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is great! Do you know if this will also be enabled for team capacity boards? We’d love to be able to see the item name (main task) above the subtasks. As of now you can’t tell which subtasks are associated with what item. Thank you!

Screenshot 2023-12-06 at 7.33.00 PM1076×414 35.7 KB

posts.author: Regan (Regan Ludwar)
posts.cookedHtml: @InbalHorn Can you please share the My-Work roadmap.

posts.author: JSambolin (Jennifer Sambolin)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you! I would prefer to have the option of keeping the group columns as well. Sometimes these are great to search/filter by! BUT I truly appreciate the parent item issue being resolved.

posts.author: emillie-altitudehome (Emillie)
posts.cookedHtml: i am seeking this feature as well. on ‘my work’ we have no idea what parent item the subitem belongs to… and when you click on the board relevant to it it jsut takes you to the ‘updates’ section and its very hard to know where the sub item belongs or to even find it.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml: Hi community, I am excited to share the ability to view parent items of a subitem is now available on the new My work layout! Thank you for all your feedback and helping us bring this feature to life!

posts.author: sivi (Sivan Rinat)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @Julietteb can you please share more details on how this work? Is this referring to when you hover over the subitem in My Work, and can see the parent item, or is there a clearer way?

posts.author: DavidCholley (David Christopher Holley)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ll be frank and to the point. If I were evaluating Monday as a PM tool, this would disqualify it. Project Management isn’t just about reporting which tasks are completed. It’s about the coordination of efforts across [n] departments and [n] teams. Often times the same resource is on [n] teams and [n] projects.

The failure to provide a person with the ability to see what he/she needs to work on across teams and projects demonstrates a failure to understand projects.

The underlying needs of Project Management are “As a PM, I need to know the tasks for this project, their status and the timeline to complete them”, “As a Resource, I need to know what to work on today to meet my various commitments,” and “As Manager of Resources, I need to know what is on my team’s plate to ensure that they are not overcommitted and able to meet their deadlines which I am ultimately responsible for.”

The need to know the Parent Item is core need that should have been considered when My Work was originally designed.

We should not have to vote for this. Someone with Monday should step and simply recognize the need and implement it.
